reviews.author: J M
reviews.text: So beautiful, so peaceful. This pagoda rests up above the river with a lovely view point from the hill and has tranquil gardens to stroll through. Take the time to watch the koi and check out the really cool bonsai garden. There is a path in the very back where you can see the extensive graveyard behind the wall, the family plots were very interesting and beautiful to see. Thank you for being open to the public and sharing your sacred grounds.

reviews.author: Roman Wolf
reviews.text: A profoundly serene and historically rich spiritual sanctuary. A peaceful, deeply meaningful pagoda that is great for experiencing living history, remarkable bonsai art, and a profound sense of tranquility. Note: It is a free, active religious site, so modest dress and respectful behavior are required. Best for travelers interested in culture, history, and finding a moment of peace amidst a busy itinerary. Personal Details to Remember: The awe-inspiring ancient bonsai trees (some over 500 years old), the moving tribute to a self-sacrificing monk, the powerful sense of serenity in the gardens, the legendary prophecy behind its name ("Heavenly Lady"), and the intriguing modern marriage myth. This pagoda offers an unforgettable and layered experience, combining natural beauty, deep history, and spiritual significance in one of Vietnam's most important religious sites.

reviews.author: Sarah Grund
reviews.text: It's a really nice and peaceful place to visit, especially for free! Just don't make the mistake on booking a grab to the ancient capital, but then agreeing to the Grab driver to take you to the free pagoda first - because he 100% hustled us even though we kept asking how much, how much. At this point he already drove past the capital place and said pay later. Then when we finally got to the ancient capital, he charged us 160k which i thought was a lot considering it was only supposed to be 34k to the ancient capital. Anyway, lesson learned! Overall, the pagoda is definitely worth the visit!

reviews.author: Travis Kelly
reviews.text: We visited Thiên Mụ Pagoda as part of our shore excursion from the cruise ship, and it ended up being one of the nicest stops on the tour. The pagoda sits on a hill overlooking the Perfume River, and the views alone make the visit worthwhile. The grounds are peaceful, clean, and beautifully maintained, with plenty of space to walk around and take photos. The architecture is elegant and iconic, especially the seven-story tower at the entrance. Inside the complex, the gardens, pathways, and temple buildings create a calm and serene atmosphere that feels less crowded than other major attractions. Our guide shared the history and significance of the pagoda, which added depth to the experience. Even without a guide, the cultural and spiritual presence of the site comes through clearly. Overall, Thiên Mụ Pagoda is a beautiful and relaxing place to visit in Huế. It is easy to explore, offers great river views, and is an excellent stop on any cruise tour or day trip.

reviews.author: Teemu Koponen
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful places to visit in Hue without even needing to pay for entrance. There is small fee for parking which varies on your time of arrival. Totally recommend visiting even if it's only attraction you go in Hue.

reviews.author: ProTalk English Center
reviews.text: Những góc chụp quá đẹp ở Chùa Thiên Mụ! Thiên Mụ Pagoda is one of those places you just have to visit when you're in Hue. The area is calm, peaceful, and has a beautiful view overlooking the Perfume River. The tower is stunning in real life, and the whole temple grounds feel very spiritual and relaxing. It’s a nice place to take a slow walk, enjoy the fresh air, and learn a bit about Hue’s history. Even if you’re not really into temples, this one is really worth stopping by!

reviews.author: Rogan Yeoh
reviews.text: I came here at 7am and it was peaceful and quiet. Some monks are out and about taking care of the garden, you can hear the birds chirping. Motorbike parking cost only 3000vnd.

reviews.author: Jonatan Jonsson
reviews.text: Nice temple close to central Hue, worth visiting. Very touristy, but not overwhelming. If you want attention, attend in traditional clothes, then you will have to pose with many locals haha 😄 😆

reviews.author: Chloe Dang
reviews.text: This is famous place in Hue, an ancient Pagoda, well-maintained with nice river view. It's close to the city. The Pagoda on the hill. It's pretty easy to approach. I spend one hour in this area. The trip was worthwhile. Amazing!

reviews.author: Milan Marecek
reviews.text: Must see in Hie. Very nice place. Pagoda, temple and park. There is also the car in which Quảng Đức traveled to his self-immolation in Saigon on June 1963. If you are in modern history...

reviews.author: Ruairidh Shalley
reviews.text: Seen many pagodas here in Vietnam this one is definitely worth a visit. Especially if you’re looking for that perfect vacation pic! It’s also walking distance to the old fashioned garden house and several restaurants in the area with very nice views.

reviews.author: Moonlight85
reviews.text: Thien Mu Pagoda, also known as the Pagoda of the Celestial Lady, in Hue, is the tallest pagoda in Vietnam, standing seven stories high, with each level symbolizing a stage of Buddha's reincarnation. The pagoda was built in 1601 by the first Nguyen lord and is considered the unofficial symbol of Hue city. It is located on the banks of the Perfume River and has been the site of many significant events in Vietnamese history.

reviews.author: Dat Luong
reviews.text: A quaint tourist spot. It is serene, sleepy and yet charming. This spot is especially enticing when there are not a lot of tourists around and you are left on your own. The history of this spot is already covered on other sites, so I am not going over it.

reviews.author: Monica Flux
reviews.text: We visited Thiên Mụ Pagoda (Chùa Thiên Mụ) during a trip to Hue, and it was worth the trip. The iconic seven-story Phuoc Duyen Tower stands majestically by the Perfume River, offering stunning views and perfect photo spots. We went in the morning to try and beat the morning rush of people but it was still a bit busy. Highly recommend for anyone seeking beauty, culture, and peace.

reviews.author: Shauna Finch
reviews.text: Great little place. Beware of the hassle from the sellers nearby. And boat ride touts. Not sure what the price is but ours got halved as kept following us. Happy with boat ride though

reviews.author: Leong Jonathan
reviews.text: Thien Mu Pagoda isn’t just a landmark in Hue — it’s the iconic pagoda that feels like a serene gateway back in time. Perched on a hill overlooking the Perfume River, it’s a place where history, spirituality, and stunning architecture all come together. The star of the show is, of course, the seven-story pagoda itself. Standing tall and proud, it looks like a guardian watching over the city. As you walk through the entrance with its fierce guardian statues, you can’t help but feel a little humbled — and maybe slightly judged for all your past sins (don’t worry, they seem forgiving). Inside the grounds, there’s so much to see: intricate statues, the ancient bell that’s as impressive as it is photogenic, and peaceful gardens perfect for a reflective stroll. The atmosphere is calm, even with visitors around, and it’s easy to imagine what life must have been like centuries ago when this was a bustling center of worship. Pro tip: Wear comfortable shoes — the steps are historic (translation: uneven), and take a moment to just sit quietly and enjoy the breeze by the river. It’s also a fantastic spot for photography, so bring your camera or phone fully charged. Overall, Thien Mu Pagoda is a must-visit while in Hue. Whether you’re there for the history, the architecture, or just a bit of soul-soothing calm, you’ll leave feeling like you’ve gained a little perspective… and probably a lot of great photos. 📸

reviews.author: Kathleen Jeugmans
reviews.text: Standing tall above the Perfume River, Thien feels like it’s been quietly watching Hue for centuries—serene, timeless, and just a little mysterious. The seven-tiered tower seems to touch the sky. Wander through its gardens and you’ll find peace, even as scooters buzz faintly in the distance

reviews.author: Czarina Jhannette Ventura
reviews.text: A small complex comprised of the pagoda and a temple above stone steps. The pagoda faces the perfume river, which used to be perfumed by flowers flowing down from the vicinity. The pagoda is not open for entry, but you can still admire the piece of preserved history. The temple in the back is open for visitors, but please show respect and follow the rules in terms of clothing and decorum.

reviews.author: Gaurav Agrawal
reviews.text: A Buddhist religious place in Hue city of Vietnam This place has a famous 7 storied pagoda This place is icon of city It is located near the banks of clam perfume river This place is said to be ancient while present structure was built during 16th century by Nguyen rulers This is one of the most place in Hue It has big metal bells displayed here The entry is free here It is near 4 to 5 km from imperial city of hue You can take special hop on hop off tourist bus Must visited place in hue

reviews.author: Mimi Le
reviews.text: Lovely pagoda overlooking the river. Very nice and tranquil and free to walk in/around. The grounds are much larger than it looks and in the back is a courtyard full of large bonsais. In the very back is a miniature pagoda with its own courtyard

reviews.author: hoangminh nguyen
reviews.text: A beautiful and unique place, the pagoda sits on a hill, peaceful and serene, right next to the picturesque Perfume River. There are tasty local foods and great seating spots by the river. Nearby you can enjoy kayaking, swimming in the river, and listening to the famous Hue folk songs. Inside the pagoda, many relics and valuable information about the Nguyen Dynasty (Vietnam’s last feudal dynasty) are preserved. The pagoda also houses a great bell recognized as a national treasure.

reviews.author: KenJi Mr
reviews.text: The Lover's Curse Legend tells of a tragic couple who committed suicide in the Perfume River due to family forbidden love. Local lore says the boy’s spirit remained, and now any couple visiting the pagoda will eventually break up. ​The Celestial Lady The name "Thien Mu" (Heavenly Lady) comes from a vision by Lord Nguyen Hoang. An elderly lady in red appeared on the hill, foretelling that a lord would build a pagoda there to stabilize the country's prosperity. ​The Historic Car The pagoda houses the Austin car that carried Thich Quang Duc to Saigon in 1963, where he famously self-immolated to protest religious persecution. It remains a powerful symbol of sacrifice. ​The Sacred Bell Cast in 1710, the "Dai Hong Chung" bell weighs over 2 tons. It is believed that its sound can purify the soul and reach the spiritual world, echoing across the city as a call to mindfulness.

reviews.author: Lý Phối Nghi
reviews.text: Free entry, you can park your motorcycle 🏍️ near there with 3k. After visiting you can sit next to the Huong river side and have Silken tofu in ginger syrup just 10k and bánh lọc for 20k.

reviews.author: Charlotte N
reviews.text: Situated on a hill overlooking a bend of Huong River, the historic pagoda, built in 18th century, is still magnificent and well preserved. One of the pride treasures of Vietnam.

reviews.author: Erik Blaney
reviews.text: Stunning scenery and we'll manicured gardens maintenance by Monks onsite. Lovely vibes and a must see

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: the pagoda was built during Nguyen Dynasty in the early 17th century, regarded as the most well-known pagoda in Hue. The traditional architecture style looks simple but solemn and peaceful. A bit noisy with many tourists in the morning, will be more quiet in the late afternoon or sunset time.

reviews.author: Anh Minh
reviews.text: nice experience, the view is spectacular and you guys also can try tofu with extraordinary view which near the pagoda! highly rcm

reviews.author: Sidney Plant wood
reviews.text: We loved this place. Free entry, park down the road to avoid parking fees, ignore the locals just trying to scam you.

reviews.author: Anthony Giarratano
reviews.text: This is a free attraction in Hue. Very peaceful and calm environment. Nice river view. There are several souvenir shops leading up to the stairs.

reviews.author: Alex Macfarlane
reviews.text: Beautiful pagoda right by the perfume river. Visited one late afternoon in August and it was very busy with tourists on tours. But the grounds are very peaceful if you keep walking back. Some monks were even playing badminton with public.
